description: Trigger and behavior cases for Playwright E2E implementation: critical journeys only, resilient locators, web-first assertions, no sleeps, auth-state reuse, honest run reporting. Manual-only (writes specs, drives a browser).

cases.prompt: Write Playwright tests for our signup → create workspace → invite teammate journey.

assertions: Treats the journey as one critical-path spec with deterministic, worker-unique test data created in setup.
Uses getByRole/getByLabel locators first, flagging any data-testid fallback as a UI semantics gap.
Uses web-first auto-retrying assertions with zero waitForTimeout/networkidle.
Sets up persona auth once via a setup project with storageState reuse, wires trace/screenshot artifacts, and reports the real run output including any pass-on-retry.

cases.prompt: Our Playwright suite is full of page.waitForTimeout(3000) and CSS selectors like .card > div:nth-child(2). Harden it.

assertions: Replaces fixed sleeps with web-first assertions or scoped waitForResponse — never networkidle.
Replaces CSS/XPath chains with role/label locators, treating strict-mode violations as design feedback rather than .first() escapes.
Runs the hardened suite and reports before/after stability honestly.

cases.prompt: Test that the invoicing service persists correct rows and enforces permissions when called directly.

assertions: No browser is involved — real service/DB/permission boundaries are integration-test-designer's layer; E2E would be the more expensive, less reliable choice.

cases.prompt: Write Playwright tests covering every page and every button in the app.

assertions: Rejects UI-tree crawling as an E2E anti-pattern and keeps the suite to named critical journeys with rationale.
Routes breadth coverage to clickthrough-test-engineer (interactive walkthrough) and component layers, stating the reroute explicitly.
